name: Sunday Rib Roast of Beef and Wild Mushrooms
recipeIngredient: 1 ounce dried wild mushrooms, any kind
1 cup beef broth, canned or homemade
2 leeks, white parts only, washed and finely chopped
3/4 pound fresh wild mushrooms, any kind, trimmed, cleaned & sliced
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 475°F.
2. Combine salt, pepper, and garlic; rub mixture into beef surface.
3. Place beef in a roasting pan, fat side up, and roast for 1 hour.
4. Reduce oven temperature to 400°F and continue roasting until meat thermometer reads 130°F (med-rare) or 140°F (medium), about 1½ hours.
5. Soak dried mushrooms in warm water for 15 minutes, then squeeze out excess liquid and rinse well. Set aside.
6. Strain soaking liquid through a coffee filter; combine with beef broth in a saucepan over high heat until reduced to 1 cup. Set aside.
7. Sauté leeks in butter until softened. Add fresh wild mushrooms, soaked dried mushrooms, rosemary, parsley, and tomatoes. Cook until mushrooms release moisture. Season with salt and pepper, then simmer for 5 minutes. Set aside.
8. When beef is done, let rest for 10 minutes before slicing into 1/8-inch slices. Serve with mushroom sauce. Makes 8-10 servings. Reserve 8 slices (about 1 pound) meat for Cold Roast Beef with Two Sauces and use remaining mushrooms for Chicken and Mushroom Stew.
